reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim about maximal intersecting families. `[Nonempty S]` surfaces an implicit premise — the claim "half the subsets" requires |S| ≥ 1 to make sense (half of 2^n requires n ≥ 1), and is consistent with Mathlib's `Set.Intersecting.is_max_iff_card_eq` which requires `Nontrivial (Set S)`. The hypothesis hPP quantifies over all pairs (including T=U), forcing ∅ ∉ P, which is the standard convention for intersecting families and is necessary for the count to be correct. The maximality condition hPS ("no T outside P meets all members of P") is equivalent to "P cannot be extended" given hPP: the edge case T=∅ is automatically excluded from hPS's scope since ∅ doesn't meet any nonempty set, and for T≠∅ the two formulations coincide. The conclusion |P| = 2^(|S|-1) correctly encodes "half of the subsets". No genuine semantic mismatch.
